text: Writing clean code is an essential skill for every programmer. Clean code is code that is easy to read and understand, even by someone who did not write it. One key practice is using good naming conventions. Variable and function names should clearly describe what they do, like `calculateTotalPrice` instead of `ctp`. Another important idea is to break your code into small, focused functions that each do one specific task. This makes the code easier to test and modify. Ultimately, clean code improves maintainability, meaning it is simpler to update and fix later, saving time and effort in the long run.
